commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh The words of a talebearer are like tasty morsels, and they go down into the innermost parts of the body. Hebrew, "calumniator. "He pretends to wish well to those of whom he speaks, or else to guard the company against deceit. (Calmet) "If the devil be upon the detractor's tongue, he is in the ears of those who pay attention to him. Francis de Sales) Fear, is in the Septuagint, above. The Vulgate retains both this and the new version of St.
